reason: Project architecture, product boundaries, desktop/frontend conventions, and manual media acceptance expectations.
Product requirements for microphone/output devices, input meter, tray persistence, and excluded MVP features.
Technical boundaries for Wails shell, WebView2/LiveKit media ownership, device-control risk, and tray lifecycle.
Authoritative project implementation sequence and existing Task 3 spike guidance for device/tray validation.
Business vocabulary and constraints for system tray, microphone devices, output devices, and local settings.
UI copy, component, accessibility, and explicit exclusions for member volume and tray behavior.
Decision to use Wails 3 shell and avoid Electron fallback.
Decision to keep audio capture/playback in WebView2/LiveKit and treat output switching as spike.
Local settings boundary for future device preferences and non-persistence of close-window behavior.
Testing strategy requiring automated deterministic tests plus Windows HITL media/tray acceptance.
Active desktop/frontend scaffold contracts and validation commands.
Guidance for browser API to UI state to Wails lifecycle boundaries.
Guidance to centralize media API wrappers and avoid duplicated raw payload/field handling.
